evals.prompt: Write a Server Action that deletes a post. The post ID comes from the form data.
evals.expected_output: Secure Server Action: (1) await auth() first to verify caller identity. (2) Validate all inputs with Zod schema: z.object({ postId: z.string().uuid() }). (3) Verify the caller owns the post before deletion. (4) Import 'server-only' in the module. (5) Verify Origin header to prevent CSRF. Return structured success/error response.

evals.prompt: My component renders user-provided HTML content using raw innerHTML injection.
evals.expected_output: Raw HTML injection is an XSS risk. Sanitize with DOMPurify.sanitize(userHtml) before rendering. Better: use a Markdown renderer that escapes HTML. Also: configure CSP headers in middleware.ts to prevent inline script execution as defense-in-depth. Always treat user-provided HTML as untrusted.

evals.prompt: I want to access an environment variable containing a database password in a Next.js Client Component.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No process.env with secrets in client bundles. Only NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefixed vars are safe to expose (they are baked into the client bundle). Database passwords must never be NEXT_PUBLIC_. Access secrets only in Server Components, Server Actions, Route Handlers, or middleware. Use 'server-only' to prevent accidental client imports.

should_not_trigger: Write a Go service for sending emails
Set up load balancing with Nginx
